title: Entymal
content:

{{Quote|Really now! This fixation with the continuation of your species…disgusting!|An Entymal male comments on Human love|<ref name="The Pentastar Alignment">{{JournalCite|3|The Pentastar Alignment}}</ref>}}
'''Entymals''' were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoids]] from [[Endex]], an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of many canyons. They had a hardened [[exoskeleton]], with [[Chitin/Legends|chitinous]] membranes that enabled them to glide. The species functioned as a hive, with one [[Reproduction/Legends|fertile]] [[Queen/Legends|queen]], numerous infertile [[Gender/Legends|females]], and rare male drones. The Entymal had reputations as skilled [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], but were [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] due to their strength. That slavery continued under the auspices of the [[Pentastar Alignment]].

==Biology and appearance==
The [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] Entymals were [[Limb/Legends|bipedal]] in form, their lanky frames covered in a hardened [[exoskeleton]] that averaged 1.2 to 2 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. The exoskeleton shimmered a metallic-jade [[Color/Legends|color]] in [[Star/Legends|sunlight]]. They had pronounced heads, which were small and bulbous in form, dominated by two large jewel-like [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="AE" />

[[File:Entymal-AE.png|thumb|left|150px|An Entymal walks on four limbs.]]
Extending from their abdomen to each wrist joint was a thin [[Chitin/Legends|chitinous]] membrane. It could unfold into a para-wing providing limited gliding capacity.<ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">{{CSWECite|I|238|Entymal}}</ref> Under standard gravity conditions, an Entymal could glide somewhere between 60 and 100 meters, depending on wind conditions. A large landing area was required, with an Entymal typically requiring approximately 6 meters of flat surface to make a successful running landing. Entymal hands ended in numerous fingers, and their legs were double jointed. Entymals could walk either upright or hunched over, using all four limbs for locomotion.<ref name="AE" /> Entymal commonly had spikes extending from their exoskeletons; on their heads, torso and limbs.<ref name="AE" />

==Society and culture==
Entymal society followed a classic hive arrangement of a [[Reproduction/Legends|fertile]] [[Queen/Legends|queen]] served by barren [[Gender/Legends|females]] and a court of rare male drones. Male drones coupled with the queen in an elaborate mating ritual to produce the next generation of Entymal. That ritual also served as a [[Death/Legends|death]] ritual for the males, who died shortly after mating.<ref name="AE" /> That terminal courtship gave the Entymal little capacity to comprehend the displays of affection common among other [[Species/Legends|species]]. As a result male Entymals found [[Human/Legends|Human]] romantic endeavors disquieting and disagreeable.<ref name="AE" />

As a species, Entymals were technically adept, earning an unprecedented reputation as brilliant [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] and [[Navigator/Legends|navigators]]. That was possibly due to finely honed spatial senses, enabled by their particular brain patterns.<ref name="AE" />

==History==
{{Quote|This New Republic has some queer ideas about alien rights… I'll wager they can't wait to hatch some liberation plot for the Entymals that work the gas mines around Bextar.|Elta Besk|<ref name="The Pentastar Alignment" />}}
[[File:Entymal_with_Xizor.png|thumb|right|220px|Some Entymals meet with Prince Xizor.]]
The [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] Entymals were located on [[Endex]], an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of many canyons. Once renowned as pilots throughout the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], the species suffered great oppression with the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The Empire and its corporate allies [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] thousands of hulking drones, forcibly removing them from their hive homes. They were then forced to work for [[Amber Sun Mining Corporate]] as [[scoop ship]] pilots or as [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]] [[Miner/Legends|miners]] in their gas [[Mining/Legends|mines]] on [[Bextar]]. A few Entymals managed to obtain [[Captain's Accredited License/Legends|shipping licenses]] from the [[Bureau of Ships and Services/Legends|Bureau of Ships and Services]] during that period, but otherwise, they were forbidden to leave Endex, except as slaves. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], two Entymals met with [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]], the head of the powerful crime syndicate [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]].<ref name="AE" />

After the fall of the Empire, the Entymal's subjugation continued under the auspices of the [[Pentastar Alignment]]. [[Elta Besk]], head of [[Dynamic Automata]]—which owned the Amber Sun Mining Corporation—supported the formation of the Alignment, precisely because she feared that the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] pro-freedom ideals would lead to the revolt and liberation of her Entymal workforce.<ref name="AE" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Entymal first appeared in "[[The Pentastar Alignment]]"—[[Star Wars Adventure Journal 3|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 3]]. Much of this information was then reprinted in ''[[Alien Encounters]]''. [[The Best of the Star Wars Adventure Journal, Issues 1-4|''The Best of the Star Wars Adventure Journal'', Issues 1-4]] then reprinted the "The Pentastar Alignment" picture in color. They also received an entry in the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.
